name: Roane
description: The Roane, also known as the Selkie or Seal Folk, are shapeshifting creatures of Scottish and Irish folklore. They possess the ability to transform between human and seal forms. In their seal form, they are indistinguishable from ordinary seals. However, when they shed their seal skin, they can assume human form, often appearing as incredibly beautiful men and women. The transition is typically initiated by removing and concealing their seal skin. Roane are often drawn to humans, and stories abound of humans falling in love with, and even marrying, Roane. These unions are often fraught with sadness, as the Roane retain a deep connection to the sea and the call to return to their watery home is difficult to resist. The tales serve as a poignant reminder of the allure of the unknown and the bittersweet nature of connections between different worlds. The Roane embody the mysteries of the ocean and the captivating power of transformation.
subtitle: Shapeshifting seal folk of Scottish and Irish lore
appearance: Roane appear as ordinary seals in their animal form. In human form, they are often described as exceptionally beautiful, with dark hair and eyes that reflect the depths of the ocean. They may retain subtle features hinting at their seal nature, such as webbed fingers or a lingering scent of saltwater.
habitat: Coastal regions of Scotland and Ireland, specifically remote islands and secluded beaches.
behavior: Roane are generally solitary creatures, though they may gather in small groups during mating season. They are curious and observant, often drawn to human settlements. While generally benevolent, they can be mischievous or even dangerous if provoked, especially if their seal skin is stolen.

keyMyths.title: The Selkie Wife
keyMyths.description: A fisherman finds a seal skin on the beach and hides it. A beautiful woman emerges from the sea, searching for it. The fisherman claims he found nothing but offers her shelter. They marry and have children, but one day, the children discover the hidden skin, and the woman returns to the sea, leaving her human family behind, forever longing for her return. The story highlights the conflicted nature of Roane and the enduring pull of their seal nature.

keyMyths.title: The Stolen Skin
keyMyths.description: A young man falls in love with a Roane. To keep her with him, he steals her seal skin. She is forced to remain in human form and marry him. Years later, she discovers the skin and immediately returns to the sea, leaving her husband heartbroken, a cautionary tale about the consequences of forcing a Roane to remain in human form against their will. She never returns, showing that their true home is always the sea.

keyMyths.title: The Roane's Curse
keyMyths.description: A man disrespects a group of seals, unaware that they are Roane in their animal form. The Roane curse him and his family, bringing misfortune and tragedy upon them for generations. The story emphasizes the importance of respecting nature and the consequences of mistreating the Roane, showing the power of their magic and the importance of showing them respect.

sources.author: Campbell, John Francis
sources.work: Popular Tales of the West Highlands
sources.description: A collection of Scottish folktales, including several variations of Selkie stories.
sources.text: A collection of Scottish folktales, including several variations of Selkie stories.

sources.author: Lysaght, Patricia
sources.description: While focused on Banshees, this work also discusses related figures in Irish folklore, including Selkies and their connection to the sea.
